name: Aunt Rose's Mardi Gras Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
Mix the flour, sugar, and baking powder together in a bowl. Make a well in the middle and add the eggs, milk, salt, and vanilla. Beat these together and gradually work the mixture into the flour. Roll out on a floured board to about 1/4 inch or slightly thinner if preferred.
Cut the dough into 2 x 4-inch strips and make 3 or 4 slits in the center of each strip. Fry in deep oil until golden brown. Remove from the oil, then sprinkle with powdered sugar.
HINT: These will be firmer than traditional doughnuts.
Yield: approximately 18 to 20.
